rows.source_ref: Hugh Briss, 'Swing trading with heiken ashi and stochs', ForexFactory thread #340556 (posts #1, #20; HA-doji refinement post #22 p8), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/340556-swing-trading-with-heiken-ashi-and-stochs (anonymous forum author; no verified statistics)
rows.concept: Trade in the direction of the 100-SMA trend and enter on a Heiken-Ashi colour flip back into the trend confirmed by a Stochastic (8,3,3) cross out of the pullback extreme.
rows.eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 (Heiken Ashi, 100 SMA, Stochastic 8,3,3) trend-pullback with fixed 50-pip stop and defined MM exits; deterministic, FX-cross data available via .DWX/DXZ, FTMO/DXZ-compatible.
rows.notes: One core strategy with three MM/exit variants (ONE row): (1) 50-pip SL, hold until HA flips against trend, trail stop behind price; (2) 50-pip SL + 50-pip TP, SL->+1 after +25 pips; (3) 50-pip SL, take half at +25, remainder to breakeven + trail. Entry rule: price vs 100 SMA = trend filter (long only above / short only below); wait for pullback (HA colour change against trend), HA colour flips back into trend AND stoch cross near the extreme, enter on 4H candle close. Refinement (p8, post #22): HA-doji reversal entry (small body, long wicks both ends after a smooth counter-trend pullback). Discretionary currency-strength filter (post #20) to pick among simultaneous signals; CAD/JPY-vs-oil intermarket musing (post #39-42) is a one-off observation, not mechanical. Anonymous forum author, no verified stats; qualitative 'smooth cross/pullback' language must be mechanized (stoch oversold/overbought thresholds, HA colour-change detection). Covers JPY crosses (e.g. cadjpy). Crowded FX trend-follow archetype -> MED.
